panelBody: Choose a preloaded benchmark case. The UI simulates how ExplainBench asks answerable questions from a patch explanation.
quickstart:
git clone https://github.com/your-org/explainbench.git
cd explainbench
python -m explainbench.evaluate \
  --trajectory data/agent_runs/example.jsonl \
  --questions data/questions/example.json \
  --out results/example_scores.json

examples.name: Astropy table column behavior
examples.explanation: The patch updates table column conversion so structured array data preserves the intended column representation. It changes the conversion path rather than only adjusting downstream assertions.

questions.prompt: Based on the explanation, what should replace [[MASKED]]?

examples.name: Patch effect audit
examples.explanation: The submitted patch claims the duplicate-code checker is disabled when min-similarity-lines is zero, but execution evidence can reveal whether the modified path is actually covered.
examples.context:
Effect question context:

Before patch: reproducing test fails.
After patch: reproducing test still fails because the modified function is not executed.

Question: What happens when running the test before and after applying the patch?

questions.prompt: Which answer is best supported by the execution evidence?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The test passes after applying the patch.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The patch changes the observed return value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The test still fails because the patch has no observed effect.
